title: Blindly retrying a non-idempotent request on timeout can run it twice
rule: A client timeout or 5xx does not mean the server skipped the work: the request may have committed and only the response was lost, so auto-retrying a non-idempotent request (a POST that charges, emails, or increments) can execute it twice. Make the operation idempotent with an idempotency key or dedupe, and reserve blind auto-retry for idempotent methods.
triggers: retrying a POST on timeout charged the customer twice
duplicate side effect after a request timeout retry
is it safe to retry a POST on 5xx
timeout does not mean the server skipped the request
non-idempotent request executed twice after a retry
